name: Picadillo Clasico (Classic Picadillo)
recipeIngredient: 1 large green bell pepper, cored, seeded, and chopped
1 teaspoon salt (I'd leave this out - olives are salty)
1 6 ounce jar pimientos, drained and sliced
6 slices of french bread, fried in oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Saute the onion and green bell pepper until the onion is translucent (about 3 to 5 minutes).
3. Add the garlic and saute for another 2 minutes more. This forms the sofrito.
4. Add the ground beef and break it up well, ensuring no lumps remain.
5. Stir in the tomato sauce, olives, raisins, capers, white vinegar, sugar, salt (omit if desired), and black pepper.
6. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for at least 20 minutes until slightly liquid.
7. Transfer picadillo to a serving dish and garnish with pimento slices.
8. Arrange fried bread slices around the plate.
Picadillo a la Criolla (Picadillo Creole Style): Omit the tomato sauce. After 20 minutes of simmering, stir in 4 or 5 beaten eggs and cook until scrambled.
Picadillo con Papitas Fritas (Picadillo with Fried Potatoes): While picadillo simmers, fry peeled cubed potatoes in vegetable oil over medium heat until golden brown (about 10 minutes). When done, toss the potatoes in and mix well. For less fat, add raw potatoes to the picadillo and cook until finished.
